entry.title: Washington Post: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia controls most of Severodonetsk, governor says

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 12 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Elderly rickshaw puller the main draw in secluded Yamagata spa
  • Asakusa retailers get ready to roll out the red carpet to tourists
  • Penal provisions to focus on rehabilitation not punishment
  • Japan's defence minister slams nuclear neighbours who 'ignore rules'
  • Diet gender gap needs fixing, say bulk of Lower House members
  • Kishida blasts changing status quo through use of military force
  • Shiseido shifts focus to quality products that are made in Japan
  • U.S., Chinese defence chiefs stand firm over Taiwan in first meeting
  • North Korean leader reaffirms arms buildup in party meeting
  • N.Korea's Kim vows 'power to power' military; promotes nuclear envoy
  • Zelenskyy tells Asian meeting: Stopping Russian invasion crucial for whole world
  • US: China's military activity around Taiwan threatens region
  • Researchers find why scratching worsens chronic itching problem
  • Murakami opens up on his latest translation of F. Scott Fitzgerald
  • VOX POPULI: Leaders deserve to be judged for trying, not just failed diplomacy
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-11 22:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong FCC axed human rights press awards to avoid risk of jail, chief cites concerns about independence of courts under national security law
  • DAB vice-chairman Horace Cheung likely to be picked as Hong Kong’s first deputy secretary for justice
  • Almost 40 per cent of Hong Kong’s outsourced government workers unaware of labour rights, survey finds
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief warns against suspected phishing scam after his picture appears in online adverts
  • Hong Kong’s chief justice stresses importance of senior counsel, as 5 barristers appointed to prestigious rank
  • Hong Kong has proposed banning CBD products within a year. Here’s everything you need to know
  • Hong Kong homebuyers snap up Sun Hung Kai’s Silicon Hill flats for the second weekend in a row, aiming to get ahead of higher interest rates
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong’s status at risk if it doesn’t fully open up to international travel, leader warns, as 851 new Covid-19 cases logged
  • Hong Kong protests: ‘no regrets’ over extradition bill bid, Carrie Lam insists, saying apology in 2019 was for poor communication on legislation
  • Largest international school group in Hong Kong to launch trial run for pre-nursery classes, despite city’s declining student population
  • Three steps for Hong Kong to reconnect with the world and regain its international lustre
  • More than 540,000 Hongkongers issued BN(O) passports since 2019 with most approved after UK citizenship scheme unveiled
  • Why China is likely to focus more on Central Asia as US rivalry intensifies
  • Chinese defence officials take aim at US’ ‘aggressive’ Taiwan-Ukraine comparison
  • Covid cases bounce back in Taiwan after week of declines
  • ‘Asian water tower’ is facing a worsening supply imbalance, study finds
  • Man in China who stole US$3,700 from a classmate on his wedding day, then ‘loaned’ back some stolen cash to groom gets 2 years jail
  • Nine held in Chinese city after fierce attack on women diners sends two to hospital
  • Can vaccination or drugs help defeat long Covid?
  • Canada says it’s raised issue of China’s recent ‘concerning’ aerial behaviour through diplomatic channels
  • China’s new Covid-19 normal means ‘endless testing’, bewildering system, anxiety over expired results
  • Chinese-Canadian rapper Kris Wu tried for rape by Beijing court
  • Coronavirus: China’s jobless rate could reach 2020 levels unless Beijing ‘learns’ early pandemic lessons, experts say
  • China’s hostile environment for Western tech pushed out Amazon and Airbnb, but competition remains the biggest challenge
  • US defence chief Lloyd Austin says China is getting ‘more coercive’ in its territorial claims
  • Asean should have a ‘full-time’ Myanmar special envoy with added powers, Malaysia’s Saifuddin Abdullah says
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra says it values China trade ties, urges Beijing to be ‘transparent’ about military build-up
  • India police shoot 2 during protests over BJP official’s Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • Indian ‘granfluencers’ take Instagram by storm with sex education, knitting lessons
  • Foxconn’s Vietnam workers poached as hiring battle heats up amid US-China trade tensions
  • Australia reaches US$584m settlement with France’s Naval Group for axing submarine deal
  • Marcos touts ties with ‘good friend’ Beijing as Philippines issues new South China Sea protest
  • North Korea appoints first woman foreign minister as Kim Jong-un doubles down on arms buildup
  • China’s ‘walking on two legs’ conundrum: how to balance zero-Covid and the economy?
  • As Chinese buyer drops US$61m on 20 condo units, what’s fuelling Singapore’s home prices?
  • We live in the age of nasty metaverse wars. Is there no escape from this hell?
  • In Kyiv, EU chief promises a signal on Ukraine’s candidate status bid next week
  • Spain’s Catholics want Rome to consider optional celibacy and women priests
  • On New Mexico visit, Joe Biden faces residents’ fury over government efforts to tackle fires
  • Newly discovered dinosaur was a ‘huge’ 32-ft-long predator with a crocodile face, say scientists
  • Thousands of protesters stream to Washington’s National Mall to demand gun law changes
  • Sanctions-hit Iran signs 20-year cooperation deal with Venezuela; allies vow to resist Washington
  • US judge dismisses rape lawsuit against Cristiano Ronaldo in Las Vegas
  • Nicaragua authorises entry of Russian troops, planes and ships
  • Rescuers in Italy locate 7 bodies at scorched site of helicopter crash
  • Diplomacy, punitive sanctions only useful as ‘pre-emptive’ measures to stop wars, Zelensky says at Shangri-La Dialogue
  • Joe Biden slams Big Oil over profits: ‘Exxon made more money than God this year’
  • Rare Stradivarius used in Wizard of Oz soundtrack sells for near-record US$15.3 million in New York auction
  • Joe Biden says Ukraine’s Zelensky brushed off warnings of Russia’s invasion
  • New Oriental’s Yu Minhong brings back English teaching with a live-streaming e-commerce twist
  • Coronavirus: China adds 138 new cases on Friday, as Beijing and Shanghai conduct more mass testing
  • Tesla proposes 3-to-1 stock split as Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, friend of Elon Musk, prepares to leave board
  • Coronavirus lockdowns cost China’s car sector another 400,000 vehicles in May, though ‘revenge spending’ may soon provide a boost
  • Don’t be like Johnny Depp and Amber Heard – the signs of a toxic relationship to look out for and when to walk away
  • The right swimsuit for you? It’s whatever you like regardless of your body type – low-cut or high-leg, bikini or one-piece
  • Ms Marvel directors hope Marvel’s first Muslim superhero series inspires ‘a lot of girls in the world’ as well as people of any religion, race or genders
  • Amid China’s civil war between Communists and Nationalists, how two sisters were separated by fate and kept apart through their successes
  • Breathwork, movement, sound and social interaction create ‘core steadiness’ you need to relieve stress and anxiety in the face of change, yoga therapist says
  • All of Us Are Dead, Hellbound and Taxi Driver set for season 2; Jang Na-ra, Jang Hyuk reunite for Family – K-drama casting latest
  • Up close with elephants at sanctuary in Thailand, volunteers shovelling dung and sorting mangoes gain insights on the challenges of conservation amid Covid-19 crisis
  • How global warming sparked pair’s sustainable architecture with its focus on natural ventilation and high-rise living connected to nature
  • ‘Historical nihilists’ know that the truth ultimately seeps through, eroding that which attempts to conceal it
  • Singapore’s Gillman Barracks arts centre to become lifestyle destination after several major galleries give up their leases
  • How Bruce Lee’s Way of the Dragon made Chuck Norris a star with their fight in Rome’s Colosseum, and what the film showed about Lee’s directing
  • 6 surprising food facts about Queen Elizabeth, according to a royal chef – Darren McGrady formerly cooked for her and Princess Diana, and revealed her chocolate habit and spoiling of her corgis
  • Where are the original Iron Chefs now? Before Netflix rebooted the 1990s Japanese reality show that inspired Masterchef and Hell’s Kitchen, its star chefs opened restaurants worldwide
  • Inside Jeffree Star’s controversial US$200 million net worth: the YouTuber and vegan beauty mogul bought a yak farm, splurges on hot pink Lamborghinis, fuchsia Hermès Birkins, and owns mega mansions
  • David and Victoria Beckham’s multimillion-dollar property empire: from a stylish Miami penthouse and a country estate near Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, to a mansion in London and homes in Dubai
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • [Shangri-La Dialogue] S.Korea, US, Japan to regularize trilateral exercises against NK missile threats
  • [Shangri-La Dialogue] S.Korea, US commit to flesh out plans to step up military exercises
  • S. Korean, US defense chiefs discuss stronger deterrence against NK threats
  • S. Korea to actively consider economic support for Ukraine
  • N. Korean leader urges stronger national defense; no new direct message toward US, S. Korea
  • New virus cases under 10,000 for 2nd day
  • [Interactive] Number of new child adoptees in S. Korea drops in 2021
  • [Weekender] Metaverse technologies bring health care to doorsteps
  • Summer is for buckwheat noodles
  • [Visual History of Korea] Jeju island‘s volcanic lava caves reveal Earth’s history
  • [Temple to Table] Out of grass and flowers that bud in spring: Hwajeon with spring flowers
  • [Book side] Young adult novel remembers writer Li Mirok’s life
  • [Scholars and their Spaces] ‘Ye’ makes others comfortable
  • 'The Roundup' tops 10 mln admissions in S. Korea
  • BTS' new album 'Proof' sold over 2 mln on 1st day of release
Korea Herald News articles

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Inflation Sped Up Again in May, Dashing Hopes for Relief
  • Jan. 6 Panel Puts Focus on Cabinet Discussions About Removing Trump
  • The Jan. 6 Hearing Put a True-Crime Drama on Prime-Time TV

  • Why We Still Haven’t Solved the Unpaid Internship Problem
  • With Lifeguards Scarce, It’s Beach Season That Needs Rescuing

  • Stephen Curry’s Spectacular Night Fuels Key Win Over Celtics
  • Oklahoma’s Abortion Law Raises Questions About N.C.A.A.’s Softball World Series
